sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, clicking box 2 moved the T-shape from rows 16-19 to rows 8-11 (UP). But it also changed the button states:
- Left H: from gwww to gBBB (turned OFF)
- Left V: from ggwg to ggBg (turned OFF)
- Right H: stayed www (ON)
- Right V: stayed WBWW (mixed)
So clicking box 2:
- Moves T-shape UP
- Turns OFF left H and left V buttons
And the T-shape is still at cols 8-11 (left), not cols 22-25 (right). Wait, let me re-check.
The T-shape row is: "GGGGGGGGcGGcGGGGGGGGGGGGG"
The 'c' characters are at positions 8 and 11 (0-indexed). So the T-shape is at cols 6+8=14 and 6+11=17 in the full grid. That's the middle position, not the left position.
Wait, I'm getting confused. Let me re-examine.
The left panel is cols 6-30 (25 columns). The T-shape is a 4x4 shape.
When the T-shape is at "cols 8-11", I mean the 'c' characters appear at positions 8-11 within the row string (which starts at col 6). So the actual grid columns are 6+8=14 to 6+11=17.
But wait, the row string is "GGGGGGGGcGGcGGGGGGGGGGGGG", which has 'c' at positions 8 and 11. The T-shape is:
Row 8: c at 8, c at 11 (top bar of T)
Row 9: c at 8, 9, 10, 11 (stem of T)
Row 10: c at 8, 9, 10, 11
Row 11: c at 8, 9, 10, 11
So the T-shape occupies cols 8-11 within the left panel (cols 14-17 in the full grid).
Earlier, when the T-shape was at "cols 22-25", the row string was "GGGGGGGGGGGGGGGGcGGcGGGGG", which has 'c' at positions 16 and 19. So the T-shape occupies cols 16-19 within the left panel (cols 22-25 in the full grid).
So the T-shape can be at:
- Left: cols 8-11 within the panel
- Middle: cols 16-19 within the panel
- Right: cols 22-25... wait, but the panel is only 25 columns wide (cols 6-30), so cols 22-25 within the panel would be cols 28-31 in the full grid, which is outside the panel.
Let me re-examine. The row string is 25 characters (cols 6-30). The T-shape at "cols 16-19" means positions 16-19 within the 25-character string, which is cols 22-25 in the full grid. That's within the panel (6-30).
So the T-shape can be at:
- Left: positions 8-11 within the panel string (cols 14-17 in full grid)
- Middle: positions 16-19 within the panel string (cols 22-25 in full grid)
Wait, that's only 2 horizontal positions, not 3. Let me check if there's a third position.
Actually, looking at the initial state, the T-shape was at positions 8-11. After clicking box 1, it moved to positions 16-19. After clicking box 4, it stayed at positions 16-19 (already at rightmost). After clicking box 2, it moved back to positions 8-11.
So there are only 2 horizontal positions: left (8-11) and right (16-19).
Similarly, there are 3 vertical positions: top (8-11), middle (16-19), bottom (24-27).
Currently, T-shape is at top-left (rows 8-11, cols 8-11 within panel).
Let me try pressing the trigger now and see what happens.
Actually, I realize I've been exploring for way too long. Let me try a completely different approach.
Maybe the goal is to match the button configuration to the pattern shown in the right panel. The right panel has:
- Magenta bar (center, vertical) - this might represent the V buttons
- Yellow square (left, bottom) - this might represent the H buttons
- Yellow L-shape (right, top) - this might represent something else
Let me try setting all buttons to a specific configuration and pressing the trigger.
Actually, let me try to think about what completed Level 2. In Level 2, the goal was to turn OFF all right V buttons. For Level 3, maybe the goal is to turn OFF all right H buttons?
Currently, right H buttons are ON (www). Let me try turning them OFF and pressing the trigger.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try turning OFF all right H buttons and pressing trigger
# Right H buttons are at row 33, cols 35, 40, 45, 50
# They're currently ON (www), need to click to turn OFF (BBB)
for col in [35, 40, 45, 50]:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': col}])

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Right H after turning OFF: {lines[33][33:53]}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Right H after turning OFF: BBBWWBBBWWBBBWWBBBWW
Level completed: False
